acceptance_targets: [CH10_DIALOGUE_001] Pass if the revised passage at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:73-82 still communicates that certification records said the area was clear and Haley relied on them, but no line repeats or near-repeats the calibration phrase "class-certified sin-eater, registered, verified" or its surrounding sentence structure. Fail if a cold read can still hear the style-bible example line in Caul's speech.

[CH10_CRAFT_001] Pass if, after the revised equivalents of snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:103-107 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:191-193, the next beat creates new pressure rather than re-explaining the same moral point, and the ending still preserves the must-land beats of Caul's loss, Derry's trust erosion, and Derry walking ahead. Fail if the chapter again explains that Pell/Fenhold were living in contamination without adding a new fact, decision, or cost.

[OVERSTATEMENT_CH10] Pass if line 2684 describes Derry's face through physical detail without naming the abstract emotional process, lines 2694-2698 present Derry's family-talk without explicit interpretive metaphor from the narrator, and 2708 is compressed to no more than two sentences. Fail if the scene still converts character behavior into narrator-level diagnosis of what the behavior means.
[R-03] Ch 10 contains no more than 2 occurrences of 'eleven years.' The near-duplicate sentences at L2652 and L2730 are consolidated into one.
[R-11] Ch 10 em-dash density is at or below 15% of prose lines.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised passage at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:73-82 still communicates that certification records said the area was clear and Haley relied on them, but no line repeats or near-repeats the calibration phrase "class-certified sin-eater, registered, verified" or its surrounding sentence structure. Fail if a cold read can still hear the style-bible example line in Caul's speech.
findings.problem: Caul's central accusation reuses calibration-only style-bible phrasing almost verbatim, especially the sequence about a "class-certified sin-eater, registered, verified." Because the borrowed wording sits inside the chapter's defining emotional reveal, it damages voice integrity on first read and makes the scene sound assembled from reference material instead of spoken by Caul.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rewrite Caul's explanation at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:73-82 so the same facts remain on the page (the site was logged as clear, Haley trusted the record, the dungeon should have been safe) but the sentence order, phrasing, and rhythm are newly invented for this scene. Remove the borrowed formulation at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:77 and let Caul's own ranger-report cadence carry the beat without echoing calibration text.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if, after the revised equivalents of snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:103-107 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:191-193, the next beat creates new pressure rather than re-explaining the same moral point, and the ending still preserves the must-land beats of Caul's loss, Derry's trust erosion, and Derry walking ahead. Fail if the chapter again explains that Pell/Fenhold were living in contamination without adding a new fact, decision, or cost.
findings.problem: The chapter lands its moral payload cleanly twice, then keeps translating and replaying the same realization instead of moving to a new consequence. Caul's bird-journal beat already makes the loss specific, and Derry's "Pell was living in it" already makes the local cost irreversible; after that, the narration paraphrases the chapter's meaning and the stream exchange restages daughter-plus-contamination logic without adding a new fact, choice, or operational change.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:103-121 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:187-247, trust the bird-journal reveal and the "Pell was living in it" beat as arrivals. Compress the abstract paraphrase that follows those arrivals, and either cut the stream re-argument at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:233-247 or replace it with a new consequence that changes the travel dynamic beyond restating guilt, such as Derry withholding practical cooperation, naming a next-step decision, or otherwise altering what the two men will do from here.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if line 2684 describes Derry's face through physical detail without naming the abstract emotional process, lines 2694-2698 present Derry's family-talk without explicit interpretive metaphor from the narrator, and 2708 is compressed to no more than two sentences. Fail if the scene still converts character behavior into narrator-level diagnosis of what the behavior means.

evidence.excerpt: 2684: Maren looked at Derry. The four feet between them had become architectural — not a gap but a structure, something Derry had built and placed himself on the other side of. His arms were still locked around his knees. His red-brown hair was dirty from travel and pressed flat on one side where he'd slept on it wrong. His face was doing the thing Maren had been afraid of: not anger — Derry didn't have the wiring for the kind of anger this warranted — but understanding. The slow, thorough, irreversible kind. A man looking at his oldest friend and reassembling every shared memory in light of a clearing and a ranger and two names that belonged to dead people who trusted the System to keep them safe.

2696: Maren said nothing. He understood what Derry was doing — he'd watched it for years, the retreat to Pell and Lenna when the world became too large, the family used as a sea wall against things that couldn't be faced directly. But this time the retreat didn't sound like comfort. This time when Derry talked about Pell it sounded like measurement. Like he was placing his daughter's small ordinary life on one side of a scale and watching something on the other side rise to meet it.

2708: Maren opened his mouth. The truth was right there, simple and complete: *Yes. I didn't know the details or the mechanism or that it could kill people. But I knew the ritual was empty. I knew nothing was being cleared. I didn't stop because stopping meant losing the income and the identity and the one thing I could do, which was pretend to be something I wasn't, and I was good at that and I was afraid of what I'd be without it.* The truth was right there and it was the most dangerous thing in the clearing — not because Derry would hurt him but because Derry would understand him, completely and precisely, and then Derry would forgive nothing, and Maren would deserve both the understanding and the absence of forgiveness.

2694: Derry nodded. He looked at the dirt between his feet again. Then he said, in the voice he used when he was retreating to the only safe ground he had: "Pell's going to be three next month. Lenna's been planning something — she told me before we left. She's going to make a cake with honey from the Grenning apiary. Pell's never had honey cake before." He paused. "She's counting everything now. Stairs, stones, peas in a pod. Lenna says she won't stop. She'll count anything you put in front of her."

findings.problem: After the Caul confrontation, the scene over-interprets the emotional aftermath of Derry's reckoning. At 2684, Derry's face is described through physical detail (red-brown hair, dirty) but then the passage names the abstract process: 'A man looking at his oldest friend and reassembling every shared memory in light of a clearing and a ranger and two names that belonged to dead people.' At 2696, Derry's retreat to Pell-and-Lenna talk is interpreted via explicit metaphor: 'Like he was placing his daughter's small ordinary life on one side of a scale and watching something on the other side rise to meet it.' At 2708, Maren's inability to speak the truth is elaborated across 8 lines of internal monologue that state what the scene has already made legible. In each case, the reader has already grasped the emotional content from the scene's action and dialogue; the narrator's interpretation converts subtext to text.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2684, snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2694-2698, and snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:2708. At 2684, keep the physical details of Derry's face but cut the abstract process-description ('reassembling every shared memory...'). At 2696, let Derry's Pell-talk land without Maren's interpretive metaphor ('it sounded like measurement... placing his daughter's small ordinary life on one side of a scale') — the reader already feels the weight shift. At 2708, compress the internal monologue about truth-telling: the simple fact that Maren opens his mouth and says nothing communicates the same content with more force. Trust 'Pell was living in it' (2698) to do the scene's closing work without layered narrator interpretation.

findings.acceptance_test: Ch 10 contains no more than 2 occurrences of 'eleven years.' The near-duplicate sentences at L2652 and L2730 are consolidated into one.
findings.evidence: The phrase 'eleven years' appears 8 times in Ch 10 (lines 2508-2756). Examples: L2556 'eleven years of professional accuracy'; L2652 'You have known for eleven years'; L2686 'How many villages...in eleven years'; L2712 'eleven years watching Maren build sentences'; L2730 'You have known for eleven years.' L2652 and L2730 are near-verbatim repetitions of each other within the same chapter.
findings.problem: Ch 10 re-proves the fraud-duration baseline 8 times after the reader has been told 58+ times. The Caul scene's emotional power comes from Haley and Renn's deaths and the bird drawing — not from restating the number. L2652 and L2730 duplicate the same sentence. First established Ch 1 L9.
findings.rewrite_direction: Reduce 'eleven years' in Ch 10 from 8 to at most 2. Keep Caul's direct statement about the contamination timeline (one instance). Cut intra-chapter duplication (L2652 and L2730 must not both survive). Replace other instances with 'all that time,' 'for years,' or contextual references.

findings.acceptance_test: Ch 10 em-dash density is at or below 15% of prose lines.
findings.evidence: Ch 10 has 61 em-dash-containing prose lines out of 122 total prose lines = 50.0% density. Manuscript-wide: 979/2406 = 40.7%. Threshold: 10%.
findings.problem: Em-dash density in Ch 10 is 50.0%. The Caul confrontation chapter uses em-dashes for virtually every parenthetical and aside, making the punctuation invisible through overuse. The emotional weight of Caul's story about Haley and Renn is diluted by the same punctuation gesture appearing in every other line.
findings.rewrite_direction: Reduce em-dash-containing lines in Ch 10 from 61 to no more than 18 (targeting 15% density). Caul's speech pattern should use fewer em-dashes than Maren's internal narration — his delivery is described as measured and practiced, which maps to complete sentences, not interrupted ones.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Prioritize structural and craft blockers first: causality, chapter architecture, stakes clarity, continuity logic, and all HIGH/CRITICAL defects.
revision_pass.label: HIGH/CRITICAL structural/craft fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: This novel must commit fully to its own weirdness. A System-blind con man with a dead paladin and a dead assassin arguing in his skull is inherently absurd, and the novel should let that absurdity live alongside genuine grief, genuine violence, and genuine mystery without apologizing for the tonal range. The darkest moments and the funniest moments may occupy the same scene. When they do, let both registers operate simultaneously — do not sacrifice comedy for gravitas or gravitas for comedy. Risk means specificity: the specific sensation of a body doing things you did not tell it to do, the specific comedy of a dead paladin critiquing your hygiene while you're running for your life, the specific horror of carrying incomplete people whose memories have holes, the specific shame of learning that your eleven years of fraud may have killed people. When in doubt between too raw and too safe, choose raw. The novel's emotional range should be wider than any single genre label would suggest.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Euphemism in the narration of violence, bodily experience, moral compromise, or physical consequence is a draft-level failure. Maren is a con man who has seen hard things and done dirty work in poor villages for eleven years — his narration should not flinch where he would not flinch. The style bible's sensory bias toward tactile and proprioceptive detail means physical consequence must be rendered through the body: what it feels like to throw up, to kill someone, to have your muscles operate without your consent, to perform a ritual that requires you to open yourself to contamination. Summary and abstraction at the moment of consequence is always worse than presence.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the norm for all characters except Aldric, whose low contraction level is a deliberate character marker that should erode over the course of the novel. Uncontracted forms in other characters' speech are reserved for emphasis, deliberation, or ceremony. In a world of practical people doing unglamorous work, stiff diction is the exception.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate between hot and quiet chapters as a structural principle. No two consecutive chapters should open the same way — if one opens with action, the next should open with interiority or setting; if one opens with dialogue, the next should open with physical sensation or observation. Vary scene count between chapters: compressed single-scene chapters after multi-scene ones. Let quiet chapters stay quiet — do not insert action beats or revelations to maintain pace. The pacing engine is Maren's voice and the interplay between the three internal consciousnesses, not external plot pressure.
prose_style_profile.diction: Colloquial with earned elevation. Default register is smart but not literary — a con man's English, precise about people and vague about feelings. Elevation reserved for moments of genuine awe, grief, or the rare glimpse of something beautiful. Fantasy-register portentousness is a draft-level failure — no 'ancient darkness' or 'powers beyond comprehension.' The world is weird and dangerous and ordinary all at once, and the prose should match.
forbidden_drift_patterns: Sentence fragments imitating action-movie or thriller prose — subject-dropping and article-stripping for false urgency
Semicolon-heavy scholarly narration that distances the reader from embodied experience
Present-tense micro-shifts for intensity — the novel is past tense throughout, no exceptions
Filtering words ('he saw,' 'he felt,' 'he noticed,' 'he realized') when direct rendering is available
Fantasy-voice portentousness: 'ancient,' 'dark power,' 'something stirred,' 'a presence beyond reckoning'
Comic narration that winks at the reader or breaks the fourth wall instead of committing to the situation
Repeated metaphor templates — if a comparison structure appears twice in a chapter, the second instance is a tic
Emotional summary replacing scene presence: 'He was overwhelmed by grief' instead of rendering what grief looks and feels like in the body
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, locked to Maren throughout. Aldric and Vess are experienced as internal voices — they speak as italicized dialogue within Maren's consciousness, clearly distinguished from his own thoughts. Maren cannot access their thoughts directly; he hears what they say to him, not what they think. The narration inherits Maren's sensory limitations: the System interface is always mediated through Aldric or Vess's descriptions, never rendered directly until the single exception in Chapter 17.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Varied by emotional register. Default: medium-length sentences with periodic short declaratives for grounding. When Maren is performing (lying, charming, faking): smoother, more controlled rhythm with longer subordinate-clause chains. When Maren is honest or overwhelmed: choppier, more fragmented, more concrete. Fight scenes: visceral and slightly disoriented, reflecting Maren's System-blindness — sensation and proprioception without the visual data everyone else has. Quiet scenes: allow longer, more observational sentences. The rhythm should breathe differently in every chapter.
sensory_bias: tactile — Maren's primary sense channel; the physical world is how he navigates System-blindness
auditory — the hum of System activity, Aldric and Vess's voices, the specific sounds of quest-light and contamination
proprioceptive — Maren's body doing things he didn't choose; the internal sensation of carrying fragments; the physical cost of consumption
olfactory — contamination has smell; ritual has smell; the world stinks in specific ways that the System-sighted don't notice because they navigate by interface
